CATALOG.wb: Dark Elves Heroes only (upgrades sword or dagger; +15 gc at recruitment, +20 gc later)
Marauders of Chaos (Chosen of Chaos) only
Marauders, Norse, Beastmen, Chaos Dwarfs, Possessed, Carnival only
Sisters of Sigmar & Chaos Dwarfs only
Skaven, Lizardmen, Forest Goblins only (Common for Forest Goblins)
Gunnery School of Nuln only (also Maneaters)
Marauders, Norse, Beastmen, Chaos Dwarfs, Possessed, Carnival only
Shadow Warriors only (only at warband creation)

EQEN.Skink-Stil (Helm/Dolch/Dreizack o. Javel: Skink kit (helmet/dagger/trident or javelins/net or buckler)
EQEN.Witch-Elf-Stil (Helm/Dolch/2 Schwerter o: Witch Elf kit (helmet/dagger/2 swords or spear & net)

ITEMINFO.name: Compass
ITEMINFO.text: The warband may re-roll the dice when rolling on the Exploration chart (must accept the second result), and the bearer's warband adds +1 to the roll to determine who takes the first turn.

ITEMINFO.name: Hard Tack
ITEMINFO.text: Ship's biscuit. One use: a model that eats it before the battle automatically recovers the first time it is Knocked Down that game (stands up at once).

ITEMINFO.name: Hook Hand
ITEMINFO.text: Replaces a lost hand. The wearer may not use two-handed weapons but always counts as having a close combat weapon in that hand (strikes as a dagger). If he later suffers a Hand Injury or Arm Wound, ignore it on a 4+ (the hook took the blow).

ITEMINFO.name: Jolly Roger
ITEMINFO.text: The pirate banner. Warband members within 12" of the bearer may re-roll failed All Alone tests. The banner staff counts as a two-handed weapon. Max 1 per warband.

ITEMINFO.name: Peg Leg
ITEMINFO.text: A wooden leg. The wearer's Movement is reduced by 1, but he may ignore a Leg Wound / Smashed Leg result (the peg already replaced it) and never suffers further leg injuries.

ITEMINFO.name: Spyglass
ITEMINFO.text: A model with a spyglass that did not move may add +6" to the range of any one missile weapon it fires that turn.

ITEMINFO.name: Treasure Map
ITEMINFO.text: One use. Grants the warband a large bonus in the Exploration phase (roll an extra die and add it), then the map is spent.

ITEMINFO.name: Horse
ITEMINFO.text: The rider moves 8" and may not be knocked down by charges. May not enter buildings or difficult terrain and gives no armour-save bonus (unlike a warhorse). Bretonnian Squires may only take a horse if the Questing Knight and every Knight Errant ride warhorses.

ITEMINFO.__re: lampe des dschinn|lamp of the djinn|genie lamp

ITEMINFO.name: Lamp of the Djinn
ITEMINFO.text: One use. Rub the lamp to summon a Djinn once per battle: choose a target within 12" and line of sight — it suffers a single S5 hit that ignores armour saves, OR heal one friendly model (removes Knocked Down/Stunned).

ITEMINFO.name: Flying Carpet
ITEMINFO.text: The rider may Fly: move up to 10" in any direction (ignoring terrain and models) instead of moving normally; may not carry more than one rider and may not be used to charge.

ITEMINFO.name: Monkey's Paw
ITEMINFO.text: Once per battle the bearer may re-roll any single dice roll — but the opponent then also gains one re-roll of a dice of their choice during that battle (the paw's curse).

ITEMINFO.name: Staff of the Serpent
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Strength: as user +1 · 30 gc
ITEMINFO.text: Counts as a mace/club (concussion — enemy stunned on 2-4 instead of 4). A Mystic wielding it may re-roll one failed casting per game.

ITEMINFO.name: Snake Charmer's Flute
ITEMINFO.text: At the start of the enemy turn pick one enemy within 8" and line of sight: it must pass a Leadership test or may do nothing this turn except defend itself in close combat (mesmerised). No effect on Undead or animals.

ITEMINFO.name: Magic Gourd
ITEMINFO.text: Never runs dry. The bearer automatically passes any test caused by heat/thirst/desert conditions, and once per battle may drink to remove Knocked Down (stand up immediately).

ITEMINFO.name: Nomad Robe
ITEMINFO.text: Light desert garb: gives a 6+ armour save (does not stack with other armour) and, against shooting, an additional −1 to hit as the loose robes blur the wearer's outline.

ITEMINFO.name: Scorpion Ring
ITEMINFO.text: Once per battle, in hand-to-hand, the bearer may strike with the hidden scorpion barb: one automatic S1 hit that, if it wounds, is treated as a poisoned attack (target tests as if hit by a poisoned weapon).

ITEMINFO.name: Poison Ring
ITEMINFO.text: Holds one dose. Once per battle the bearer may coat a close-combat weapon: all hits from that weapon this turn count as poisoned (+1 to wound rolls / as per poison rules).

ITEMINFO.name: Map of Nehekhara
ITEMINFO.text: Grants +1 die in the Exploration phase and lets the warband re-roll one Exploration die per game — the ancient charts reveal hidden tombs.

ITEMINFO.name: Nehekharan Javelin
ITEMINFO.line: Missile · Strength: as user · 10 gc · Common
ITEMINFO.text: Range 8", Strength as user. +1 to hit when thrown (the becket string spins the javelin like a bullet). Mordheimer note: the original only listed the +1 to hit; it is suggested to also apply the normal thrown-weapon rules (no penalty for range or moving). Tomb Lords only.

ITEMINFO.name: Aspis Arrows
ITEMINFO.text: Snake-venom arrows for any bow: any hit that wounds counts as a poisoned attack. One purchase lasts the whole battle.

ITEMINFO.name: Bota (Waterskin)
ITEMINFO.text: The bearer ignores penalties from heat and desert conditions and may re-roll a failed test caused by thirst/exhaustion once per battle.

ITEMINFO.name: Tufenk
ITEMINFO.text: Early Araby handgun. Range 24"; may not move and fire; Save modifier −2; prepare-shot (fires every other turn). Counts as a black powder weapon.

ITEMINFO.name: Skeletal Chariot
ITEMINFO.text: 0-1 per warband. Bone chariot pulled by two Skeleton Steeds, ridden by a warband member (Chariot S4 T4 W3; Steed M8 WS2 S3 T3 W1 I2 A1 Ld5). Moves 8", may not run, but doubles its move when charging. Charging: may charge any visible enemy in the open; anyone in its path takes an Initiative test to dodge or suffers 1 wound at S4 with -2 armour save; the charioteer then fights normally. Difficult ground: D3 S4 hits (2D3 S6 if charging). If one steed dies: half move, no impact hits; both dead: immobile. Shooting at it: +1 to hit (large); D6: 1-2 steed, 3-4 chariot, 5-6 charioteer. The Drive Chariot skill (Academic) is required to charge with it.

ITEMINFO.name: Conch Shell Horn
ITEMINFO.text: Piranha Warriors only. At the start of the game the warband may re-roll the roll to see who deploys first and who takes the first turn. Multiple horns cannot force a second re-roll.

ITEMINFO.name: Enchanted Skins (Skins & Charms)
ITEMINFO.text: Amazons only. Magical armour granting a 6+ armour save AND a special 5+ save to dispel spells targeting the wearer. Counts as light armour for movement (no penalty). May be combined with a shield/buckler.

ITEMINFO.name: Liber Bubonicus
ITEMINFO.text: One use, max 1 per campaign. A Pestilens Sorcerer permanently learns an additional random Horned Rat spell. A Plague Priest with the Magical Aptitude (Arcane Lore) skill becomes a spellcaster (Horned Rat) and learns a random spell. Replaces the Tome of Magic for Clan Pestilens.

ITEMINFO.name: Liturgicus Infecticus
ITEMINFO.text: At the start of a turn or just before a Rout test the warband may chant: +1 Leadership until the end of the turn. Replaces the War horn for Clan Pestilens.

ITEMINFO.name: Clan Pestilens Banner
ITEMINFO.text: Models within 12" of the standard bearer may reroll one failed All Alone test each. The banner staff counts as a two-handed weapon. Max 1 per warband. Replaces the normal Banner for Clan Pestilens.

ITEMINFO.name: Fog-enhancing Warpstone Shards
ITEMINFO.text: Put into a censer: the wielder becomes a difficult target — enemy missile attacks against him suffer −1 to hit. Replaces the Elven Cloak for Clan Pestilens.

ITEMINFO.name: Vial of Pestilens
ITEMINFO.text: One use. When the Skaven is taken Out of Action in close combat, shove the vial in the attacker's face: the attacker must roll equal or under his Toughness or is taken Out of Action (no save); in that case the Skaven is only Stunned instead.

ITEMINFO.name: Scroll of the Rat Familiar
ITEMINFO.text: Pestilens Sorcerer only, reusable. Before battle, transform one Giant Rat into a Rat Familiar (henchman, gains exp; profile like Giant Rat). If the Familiar is within 6", the sorcerer may reroll one casting roll per game; advancement 10–12 gives cumulative +1 casting instead of promotion. Max 1 Familiar. Replaces the normal Familiar.

ITEMINFO.name: Warpstone Amulet
ITEMINFO.text: Reroll a single die during the battle or — if not out of action at game end — a single die when searching for wyrdstone. Replaces the Rabbit's Foot for Clan Pestilens.

ITEMINFO.name: Kitchen knife
ITEMINFO.text: Enemy gets +1 to its armour save (6+ if it has none). Master Chef utensil. (Mootlanders)

ITEMINFO.name: Cleaver
ITEMINFO.text: Cuts like an axe: enemy −1 to its armour save. Master Chef utensil. (Mootlanders)

ITEMINFO.name: Ladle
ITEMINFO.text: No armour save except from shields or skills. Knuckle Cracking: on a to-hit roll of 6 the enemy drops his weapon. Master Chef utensil. (Mootlanders)

ITEMINFO.name: Tenderiser
ITEMINFO.text: Stuns the target on an injury roll of 2-4. Master Chef utensil. (Mootlanders)

ITEMINFO.name: Cooking pot helmet
ITEMINFO.text: Special 5+ save against being Stunned (never modified). (Mootlanders)

ITEMINFO.name: Repeater handgun
ITEMINFO.text: Move or fire. Fire thrice: up to 3 shots (−1 to hit if more than one); later shots within 3" of the previous target. Experimental (always uses black-powder misfire rules). Slow reload: a turn doing nothing to reload. (Gunnery)

ITEMINFO.name: Repeater pistol
ITEMINFO.text: Fire thrice: up to 3 shots (−1 to hit if more than one); later shots within 3" of the previous. Experimental (black-powder misfire rules). Quick reload: always fires at least one shot; needs an idle phase before firing multiple again. Not a club: usable as a pistol only in the first combat round, then no extra-hand-weapon bonus. (Gunnery)

ITEMINFO.name: Double-barrelled duelling pistol
ITEMINFO.text: Accuracy: +1 to hit (shooting and close combat). Prepare shot: every 2nd turn (brace: every turn). Hand-to-hand: +1 attack at S4, −2 save. Double-barrelled: fire one or both barrels; firing both = two wound rolls, then 2 reload tokens. (Gunnery)

ITEMINFO.name: Double-barrelled handgun
ITEMINFO.text: Prepare shot (every 2nd turn). Move or fire. Double-barrelled: fire one or both barrels; firing both = two wound rolls, then 2 reload tokens. (Gunnery)

ITEMINFO.name: Double-barrelled pistol
ITEMINFO.text: Prepare shot: every 2nd turn (brace: every turn). Hand-to-hand: +1 attack at S4, −2 save. Double-barrelled: fire one or both barrels; firing both = two wound rolls, then 2 reload tokens. (Gunnery)

ITEMINFO.name: Throwing axes
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown: no penalty for range or movement. Cannot be used in close combat.

ITEMINFO.name: Throwing knives/stars
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown: no penalty for range or movement. Cannot be used in close combat.

ITEMINFO.name: Javelin
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown: no movement penalty.

ITEMINFO.name: Blowpipe
ITEMINFO.text: Poison: a to-hit roll of 6 = automatic wound (no critical hits). Stealthy: may fire while hidden. (Skaven)

ITEMINFO.name: Warplock pistol
ITEMINFO.text: Fires every other turn (brace: every turn). Skaven only.

ITEMINFO.name: Repeater crossbow
ITEMINFO.text: Fire twice: may fire twice per turn, both shots at −1 to hit.

ITEMINFO.name: Crossbow pistol
ITEMINFO.text: May shoot in the first close-combat round (−2 to hit, resolved first) — in addition to the close-combat attacks.

ITEMINFO.name: Crossbow
ITEMINFO.text: Move or fire: may not move and fire in the same turn (turning/standing up allowed).

ITEMINFO.name: Duelling pistol
ITEMINFO.text: Accuracy: +1 to hit (also in close combat). Prepare shot: every 2nd turn (brace: every turn). Also usable in close combat.

ITEMINFO.name: Pistol
ITEMINFO.text: Prepare shot: every 2nd turn (brace: every turn). Close combat: +1 attack at S4 / Save −2, once per combat (brace: 2 attacks in the first round).

ITEMINFO.name: Handgun
ITEMINFO.text: Move or fire. Prepare shot: every 2nd turn.

ITEMINFO.name: Hochland long rifle
ITEMINFO.text: Move or fire. Prepare shot (every 2nd turn). Pick target: may shoot any visible target, not just the nearest.

ITEMINFO.name: Blunderbuss
ITEMINFO.text: Draw a straight line 16" long and 1" wide from the firer; every model in its path is automatically hit at Strength 3. Chaos Dwarf version: "Prepare Shot" — takes a turn to reload, so it fires every other turn.

ITEMINFO.name: Death-Cap Daggers
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Common
ITEMINFO.text: A pair of daggers coated in Death Cap mushroom juice (re-coated free after every game). Paired: +1 Attack. Poisoned: same effect as Black Lotus (a 6 to hit wounds automatically; you may still roll for a critical). +1 enemy armour save (counts as daggers; 6+ if the enemy had none).

ITEMINFO.name: Poisoned Daggers (Hobgoblin)
ITEMINFO.text: Used as a pair (+1 Attack for the off-hand). Swift: count as +1 Initiative when determining combat order. Permanently coated in black lotus (poison). Count as daggers, so a wounded enemy gets +1 to its armour save (6+ if it had none).

ITEMINFO.name: Crimson Shade
ITEMINFO.text: Leaves of the Estalian blood oak. Effect (one game): +D3 Initiative and +1 Movement and Strength. No effect on Undead or the Possessed. Side effect: after the battle roll 2D6 — on 2-3 the model becomes addicted (you must buy a new batch before each game or he leaves); on 12 his Initiative is increased permanently by +1.

ITEMINFO.name: Rope & Hook
ITEMINFO.text: A warrior equipped with a rope and hook may re-roll a failed Initiative test when climbing up or down. Only one rope and hook may be carried per model.

ITEMINFO.name: Misericordia
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Rare 9
ITEMINFO.text: A thin Tilean dagger. Coup de Grace: when attacking a knocked-down opponent it ignores all armour saves. (In the Lustrian Reavers list it instead gives the enemy +1 armour save normally, but rolls 2D6 to wound and keeps the highest against knocked-down foes.) Counts as a dagger.

ITEMINFO.name: Bec de Corbin
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user +1 · Common
ITEMINFO.text: Two-handed (no shield, buckler or extra weapon; a carried shield still gives +1 save vs shooting). Strike First: strikes in Initiative order even when charged. Concussion: a 2-4 on the Injury chart counts as stunned (Dwarfs are immune).

ITEMINFO.name: Ogre Club
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Common
ITEMINFO.text: Concussion: a 2-4 on the Injury chart counts as stunned. Crushing Attack (used two-handed): −1 to the enemy's armour save, and counts as +1 Strength for the defender's parry attempts (so a weaker model cannot parry it).

ITEMINFO.name: Iron Fist
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Common
ITEMINFO.text: Parry: may parry like a buckler. Gloved: nothing else may be held in that hand (no double-handed weapon); two iron fists mean no other close-combat weapons. Dual-role: works as buckler and blade together — re-roll failed parries if paired with a sword or a second iron fist.

ITEMINFO.name: Harpoon Crossbow
ITEMINFO.text: Move or fire: may not move and shoot in the same turn. Prepare shot: needs a full turn to prepare before it can fire.

ITEMINFO.name: Gnoblar Fighter
ITEMINFO.text: A claimed Gnoblar pet (treated as equipment, not a model). Adds a meagre extra body in the fight. If its Ogre goes out of action, roll D6 per Gnoblar — on 1-2 it is lost. An Ogre may own up to two different Claimed Gnoblars.

ITEMINFO.name: Lookout-Gnoblar
ITEMINFO.text: Claimed Gnoblar (equipment, not a model). The owning Ogre gains the Dodge skill (Speed list). On the Ogre going out of action, roll D6 — 1-2 the Gnoblar is lost.

ITEMINFO.name: Luck-Gnoblar
ITEMINFO.text: Claimed Gnoblar (equipment, not a model). The owning Ogre may re-roll one dice during the battle (never re-roll a re-roll). On going out of action, roll D6 — 1-2 lost.

ITEMINFO.name: Sword-Gnoblar
ITEMINFO.text: Claimed Gnoblar (equipment, not a model). Gives the owning Ogre one extra Strength 2 attack at the owner's WS, made at the same time and against a model the Ogre is already attacking. On going out of action, roll D6 — 1-2 lost.

ITEMINFO.name: Masterwork Heavy Armour
ITEMINFO.text: Ornate Tilean heavy armour granting a 4+ armour save, but it is so heavy it imposes −1 Movement even without a shield.

ITEMINFO.name: Firepots Miragliano
ITEMINFO.text: Awkward Thrown Weapon: no penalty for range, but −1 to hit if used after moving. Smoke: on a hit the target takes one S2 hit and must, at the start of its next turn, roll under its Initiative to see through the smoke — on a failure it cannot charge or shoot that turn (it may otherwise move and fight normally).

ITEMINFO.name: Lizardmen Skin Cloak
ITEMINFO.text: Grants a 6+ ward save (separate from armour) that is not reduced by enemy Strength or any other modifier.

ITEMINFO.name: Skull Mask
ITEMINFO.text: Adorned with the skulls of slain Saurus — the wearer causes Fear.

ITEMINFO.name: Lizardman Sword (upgrade)
ITEMINFO.text: Upgrades any one Sword: its envenomed edge gives +1 to wound rolls (Poison).

ITEMINFO.name: Trophy Slann Holy Headdress
ITEMINFO.text: A sacred headdress taken from a slain Slann — grants a +2 bonus to armour saves.

ITEMINFO.name: Boar Spear
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user +1 · Rare 10 · Aristocrat only
ITEMINFO.text: Strike First (first turn of combat, even when charged). Cavalry Bonus (+1 S on the charge if mounted). Cross Guard (the first assailant has −1 Attack, min 1). Unwieldy (only a shield or buckler in the other hand; no second weapon).

ITEMINFO.name: Cathayan Quilted Silk Armour
ITEMINFO.text: +1 to armour saves against any type of attack; may be combined with any other armour.

ITEMINFO.name: Bird of Prey
ITEMINFO.text: Used in the shooting phase like a missile weapon: 18" range, no line of sight needed (but not vs hidden models), ignores penalties for moving, long range and cover. Hits using the owner's BS; a hit causes a Strength 4 wound.

ITEMINFO.name: Nightmare
ITEMINFO.text: Undead destrier (M8 WS2 BS0 S3 T3 W1 I2 A1 Ld5). May Not Run (but may charge). Immune to Poison and Psychology — never tests Ld and stands still if leaderless; if the rider is wounded, roll on the Whoa Boy! table.

ITEMINFO.__re: banner des adelshauses|banner of the noble house

ITEMINFO.name: Banner of the Noble House
ITEMINFO.text: +1 Leadership to the bearer (max 10). One hand must hold it aloft: the bearer cannot use a shield, buckler, double-handed weapon or additional weapon.

ITEMINFO.name: Cymbals
ITEMINFO.text: Beaten to the tune of the Danse Macabre: any enemy model within 6" suffers −1 BS and −1 Leadership.

ITEMINFO.name: Sun King Mask
ITEMINFO.text: The wearer can never be Knocked Down or Stunned, whether by an effect or a result on the Injury table.

ITEMINFO.name: Silver Death Mask
ITEMINFO.text: If the wearer is taken Out of Action, he may re-roll any Serious Injury result; the second result stands even if worse.

ITEMINFO.name: Fish Head Mask
ITEMINFO.text: Each time the wearer takes an opponent Out of Action he regains a lost wound, or gains an extra wound (max 5) lasting until the end of the battle.

ITEMINFO.name: Faceless Mask
ITEMINFO.text: Once per game, may stop one model from intercepting his charge. Models immune to psychology (Undead, Flagellants) are unaffected.

ITEMINFO.name: Plague Doctor Mask
ITEMINFO.line: Cursed Mask · 45 gc · Twisted Scholar only
ITEMINFO.text: Once per game in the shooting phase, release a growing black cloud: turn 1 hits enemies within 3" at Strength 1, then +1" range and +1 Strength each Cavalcade turn (up to 7"/Strength 5). Roll to hit (no range/cover penalties) and to wound; cannot cause critical hits.

ITEMINFO.name: Evil Jester Mask
ITEMINFO.text: At the start of the battle roll a D6: 1 = subject to Stupidity all battle; 2-5 = Hatred of all enemies; 6 = subject to Frenzy.

ITEMINFO.name: Slaaneshi Man-Catcher
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user +1 · Rare 10 · Whipmaster only
ITEMINFO.text: Two-handed. Lock: an unsaved wound knocks the target down (no injury roll) and it cannot stand or leave the combat while the wielder keeps hold; the wielder may move and drag it (unless engaged by others). No effect on Large creatures or steeds. If still pinned at the end of the battle the target is captured (as a 61 on the Serious Injury chart, even a Henchman).

ITEMINFO.name: Hedonist Whip
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Common · Heroes only
ITEMINFO.text: Cannot be parried (no sword/buckler parries against it). Whipcrack: +1 Attack when the wielder charges, or +1 Attack that strikes first against a charger. With two whips, the off-hand gives +1 Attack but only the first whip grants the Whipcrack.

ITEMINFO.name: Man-catcher
ITEMINFO.text: Strength as user, two-handed. Capture: a model taken out of action by it becomes captured (no Serious Injury roll) and is locked in the Engine of Chaos; without an Engine, roll Serious Injuries as normal. Large models and animals cannot be captured.

ITEMINFO.name: Mechanical Suit
ITEMINFO.text: A walking engine that carries a Sorcerer afflicted by the Curse of Stone: +3 Movement. Does not hinder spellcasting; no movement penalty when combined with a shield (but not with a shield/buckler without the right skill).

ITEMINFO.name: Engine of Chaos
ITEMINFO.text: A daemon-powered prison wagon (follows the Wagon rules). Holds up to six captives (large creatures count as two). Required to cast the Sacrificial Ritual. Models captured via the Captured injury result or a Man-catcher are locked inside; their gear is lost to the warband. Starting price 125 gc (195 gc / Rare 10 afterwards).

ITEMINFO.name: Dragon Sword (Katana)
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user +1 · Rare 10
ITEMINFO.text: Two-handed: no shield, buckler or extra weapon (but +1 armour save vs ranged if carrying a shield). Parry: like a sword, roll a D6 — if higher than the opponent's best to-hit, the blow is discarded (cannot parry attacks of ≥ 2× your Strength).

ITEMINFO.name: Cathayan Longsword (Jintachi)
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Rare 12 · Emissary only
ITEMINFO.text: Mastercrafted: gives the bearer +1 Initiative and +1 Weapon Skill. Cutting Edge: −1 to the enemy's armour save. Cost 75 + 2D6 gc.

ITEMINFO.name: Quarter Staff
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Common
ITEMINFO.text: Balanced: +1 Initiative in close combat. Parry: may parry enemy blows. Freestyle: cannot be combined with another weapon/shield/buckler, but may be combined with a Monk's bare-hand attacks (so still +1 Attack).

ITEMINFO.name: Chain Sticks
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Rare 7
ITEMINFO.text: Flurry: +2 Attacks, but only in the first turn of each hand-to-hand combat; otherwise counts as two hand weapons. Two-handed: requires both hands.

ITEMINFO.name: Fish-Hook Shot
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown Weapon: no penalties for range or moving. Precise: may target models in close combat (but useless while the wielder himself is in combat). Caused Fall: instead of damage, hit then pass a Strength test (+1 vs large) to knock the target down.

ITEMINFO.name: Throwing Stars
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown Weapon: no penalties for range or moving (perfectly balanced). May not be used in close combat.

ITEMINFO.name: Cathayan Silk Cloak
ITEMINFO.text: A finely woven silk cloak. A warrior aiming a missile weapon at the wearer suffers −1 to hit.

ITEMINFO.name: Horse
ITEMINFO.text: A riding horse (the rider needs a Ride Horse / Ride Warhorse skill). Greatly increases Movement; the rider follows the mounted-model and "Whoa Boy!" rules.

ITEMINFO.name: Broadsword
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user +1 · Common
ITEMINFO.text: Difficult to use: no second weapon or buckler in the off-hand (a shield or kite shield is fine). Strike last: so heavy the wielder always strikes last, even charging (the Strongman skill negates this, like a double-handed weapon).

ITEMINFO.name: Shortsword
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Common
ITEMINFO.text: Parry: may parry like a sword (roll a D6 vs the opponent's best to-hit; not vs ≥ 2× Strength). +1 enemy armour save: a model wounded by it gets +1 to its armour save (6+ if it had none).

ITEMINFO.name: Kite Shield
ITEMINFO.text: Basic save 5+ on foot, 6+ mounted; if already wearing armour it instead adds +2 to the save on foot and +1 mounted (never better than 1+). Can be used for Shield Bash.

ITEMINFO.name: Holy Relic (Battle Pilgrim)
ITEMINFO.text: A relic of a fallen Grail Knight. A Battle Pilgrim carrying it gains Frenzy (and while frenzied is immune to Hatred). Henchmen may take it as an exception; a relic-bearer splits off into his own henchman group if there are not enough relics for the whole group.

ITEMINFO.name: Sling
ITEMINFO.text: Fire twice at half range: fire twice if you did not move — then max. 9" and each at −1 to hit.

ITEMINFO.name: Lucky charm
ITEMINFO.text: The first time the model is hit in a battle, roll a D6: on 4+ the hit is discarded and no damage is suffered. Carrying two or more charms gives no extra benefit — only the first hit may be discarded. (FAQ)

ITEMINFO.name: Halfling cookbook
ITEMINFO.text: Increases the maximum warband size by +1 (one extra member may be recruited). Not cumulative — multiple cookbooks give no further bonus — but it DOES stack with a Halfling Scout Hired Sword (+2 total). The extra member is kept even if the hero carrying the cookbook is taken out of action or dies. FAQ errata: may NOT be used by an Undead or Carnival of Chaos warband. (Note: Halfling warbands gain no benefit — they already know these recipes.)

ITEMINFO.name: Short bow
ITEMINFO.text: Short, weak bows — cheap and easy to handle.

ITEMINFO.name: Long bow
ITEMINFO.text: Greater range than the normal bow.

ITEMINFO.name: Elf bow
ITEMINFO.text: Masterful elven bow with great range.

ITEMINFO.name: Bow
ITEMINFO.text: The standard missile weapon of the Old World.

ITEMINFO.name: Starsword
ITEMINFO.text: May parry. Ignores all armour saves (ward/dodge saves still possible). Amazon special weapon. (Lustria)

ITEMINFO.name: Starblade
ITEMINFO.text: Amazonian dagger: enemy gets +1 to its armour save, but the Starblade may parry the first successful hit of any combat on a 4+. Amazon special weapon. (Lustria)

ITEMINFO.name: Sun staff (Lustria)
ITEMINFO.text: Sunbolt: a 12" shooting attack at Strength 4. Aside from ward/dodge saves it ignores armour saves and the −1 long-range penalty. Town Cryer #15 version. (Lustria)

ITEMINFO.name: Sun staff (Sunstaff)
ITEMINFO.text: Accurate: no −1 for long range. No Save: ignores any armour save. Amazons.

ITEMINFO.name: Sun gauntlet
ITEMINFO.text: Accurate (no −1 for long range). No Save: ignores armour. Hand-to-hand: also in close combat (S4, no save) — every turn, as there is no "prepare shot". Amazons.

ITEMINFO.__re: claw of the old|klaue der alten

ITEMINFO.name: Claw of the Old Ones
ITEMINFO.text: Strength: as user +1. No Save: ignores any armour save. Parry: as a sword.

ITEMINFO.name: Fighting claws (pair)
ITEMINFO.text: Pair: +1 attack. Climb: +1 Initiative on climbing tests. Parry: parries like sword+buckler (re-roll one failed parry). Cumbersome: no other weapons for the whole combat. (Skaven)

ITEMINFO.name: Weeping blades (pair)
ITEMINFO.text: Pair: +1 attack. Venomous: poisoned blades. Parry: parries like a sword. (Skaven)

ITEMINFO.name: Spiked gauntlet
ITEMINFO.text: Counts as an additional hand weapon (+1 attack) and at the same time as a buckler (parry). (rulebook p.148)

ITEMINFO.name: Trident
ITEMINFO.text: Strike First: strikes first in the first close-combat round. Parry: parries like a sword. (Pit Fighters / Pursuer)

ITEMINFO.name: Boat hook
ITEMINFO.text: Strike First in the first round (regardless of who charged). Two-handed: no other weapon/shield/buckler in close combat. (Pirates)

ITEMINFO.name: Cat o' nine tails
ITEMINFO.text: Cannot be parried (like the steel whip, incl. Whipcrack +1 attack on the charge). But the enemy gets +1 to the armour save (rope, not steel). Heroes only. (Pirates)

ITEMINFO.name: Belaying pin
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown: no range penalty (but −1 after moving). Enemy +1 to the armour save (like fist/dagger). (Pirates)

ITEMINFO.name: Swivel gun
ITEMINFO.text: Move or fire, Prepare shot, Cumbersome (−1 I, −1 M); never fire twice/after moving; always uses the black powder misfire rules. Ammunition: Ball Shot 36"/S5/−2 (Concussion); Chain Shot 24"/S4/−1 (Knocked Down on 4+); Grape Shot 24"/S3 (D6 extra hits within 4"). Gunner only, 1 per warband.

ITEMINFO.name: Pike
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Str. as user · Rare 8
ITEMINFO.text: Strike First: strikes first in the first turn of close combat (+1 Initiative that turn for the long shaft). Two-handed: no shield, buckler or additional weapon in close combat (a carried shield still gives +1 save vs shooting).

ITEMINFO.name: Rapier
ITEMINFO.text: Parry: parries like a sword. Barrage: if you hit but fail to wound, you may immediately attack again (each at −1 to hit, until you miss). Enemy +1 to the armour save.

ITEMINFO.name: Main gauche
ITEMINFO.text: Parry (like a sword); enemy +1 to the armour save. Duellist's dagger. (Hochland Bandits)

ITEMINFO.name: Choppa
ITEMINFO.text: Counts as a morning star: +1 Strength only in the first close-combat round; no second weapon/buckler in the other hand (shield allowed). (Orcs)

ITEMINFO.name: Boss Pole
ITEMINFO.text: The hero and all Goblin henchmen within 6" ignore Animosity. Acts like a spear in close combat (Strike First, mounted +1 S on the charge). (Forest Goblins)

ITEMINFO.name: Censer
ITEMINFO.text: Heavy: +2 only in the first round. Two-handed. Fog of Death: a model hit takes a Toughness test or suffers an additional automatic wound (a 6 always wounds) — the bearer tests too. Undead/Possessed immune. (Skaven Pestilens)

ITEMINFO.name: Hand-held mortar
ITEMINFO.text: Prepared shot, Move or fire, save modifier. Scatter: on a miss the shot lands 2D6" in a random direction. Explosive radius: target + all models within 1½" each take 1 S4 hit. Experimental (always uses black powder misfire rules). (Gunnery)

ITEMINFO.name: Pigeon bombs
ITEMINFO.text: Move or fire, explosive radius (target + 1½" each S4). Temperamental: no BS — D6: 5–6 hits, 2–4 fizzles, 1 explodes in hand (bearer + 1½" each S4). Supply lasts the whole game. (Gunnery)

ITEMINFO.name: Ithilmar weapon
ITEMINFO.text: +1 Initiative in close combat. Costs three times the normal weapon.

ITEMINFO.name: Gromril weapon
ITEMINFO.text: Additional save modifier −1. Costs four times the normal weapon.

ITEMINFO.name: Double-handed weapon
ITEMINFO.text: Two-handed: no shield/buckler/second weapon (a shield still counts +1 vs shooting). Strike last: always strikes last, even when charging.

ITEMINFO.name: Halberd
ITEMINFO.text: Two-handed: no shield/buckler/second weapon (a shield still counts +1 vs shooting).

ITEMINFO.name: Morning star
ITEMINFO.text: Heavy: +1 Strength only in the first close-combat round. Difficult to use: no second weapon/buckler in the other hand (shield allowed).

ITEMINFO.name: Flail
ITEMINFO.text: Heavy: +2 Strength only in the first close-combat round. Two-handed: no shield/buckler/second weapon (a shield still counts +1 vs shooting).

ITEMINFO.name: Lance
ITEMINFO.line: Close combat · Strength: +2 on the charge
ITEMINFO.text: Mounted only (needs a warhorse). +2 Strength on the charge, that round only.

ITEMINFO.name: Spear
ITEMINFO.text: Strike first: strikes first in the first close-combat round. Unwieldy: only a shield/buckler in the other hand. Mounted: +1 Strength on the charge.

ITEMINFO.name: Sword
ITEMINFO.text: Parry: when hit, roll a D6; if higher than the opponent's highest to-hit roll, the blow is parried (one parry per close-combat phase). Attacks with ≥ 2× the user's own Strength cannot be parried.

ITEMINFO.name: Bolas
ITEMINFO.text: Once per battle (returns automatically afterwards). Dangerous: a natural 1 to hit → S3 hit on the thrower. Entangle: a hit causes no wound, but the target cannot move and has −2 WS in close combat (may still shoot); frees itself in the recovery phase on 4+. (Lizardmen/Amazons)

ITEMINFO.name: Tufenk
ITEMINFO.text: Fire: on a hit roll a D6 — 4+ sets the target alight (recovery 4+ to put out, else S4 hit + may only move). Vs dry targets (mummies) S3, ignites on 2+. Prepare shot: fires only every 2nd turn. (Khemri)

ITEMINFO.name: Asp arrows
ITEMINFO.text: Made of mummified venomous snakes, magically guided: +1 to hit. (Tomb Guardians)

ITEMINFO.name: Dark Elf blade
ITEMINFO.line: Close-combat upgrade (+15 gc recruit / +20 gc later)
ITEMINFO.text: Upgrade for an existing sword or dagger; the weapon keeps its abilities (an upgraded sword still parries, an upgraded dagger still gives the enemy +1 to the armour save / 6+). Critical Damage: +1 on the critical-hit table. Wicked Edge: an injury roll of 2–4 = stunned. Heroes only. (Dark Elves)

ITEMINFO.name: Katar
ITEMINFO.text: Arabian punch dagger: −1 to the enemy's armour save. (Khemri)

ITEMINFO.name: Serpent staff
ITEMINFO.text: Two-handed, parries. Alternatively sacrifice all your own attacks/parries to use the staff's power: then always strikes first and makes 1 attack at WS4 and S4. (Tomb Guardians Liche Priest)

ITEMINFO.name: Beastlash
ITEMINFO.text: Beastbane: causes fear in animals — an animal that wants to/will charge the bearer must first pass a fear test. Cannot be parried (sword/buckler). Whipcrack: +1 attack when charging or against the charger ("strike first"). Dark Elf Beastmaster only.

ITEMINFO.name: Great Axe
ITEMINFO.text: Two-handed. Strike Last: always strikes last (even when charging), except with the Strongman skill. Cutting Edge: additional armour penalty −1. Heroes with "Chosen of Chaos" only. (Marauders)

ITEMINFO.name: Barbed Whip
ITEMINFO.text: Cannot be parried (no sword/buckler parry). Whipcrack: like the steel whip (+1 attack when charging/against the charger, "strike first"). Enrage: as long as the hero is not himself in close combat, all Warhounds of Chaos within 4" get +1 attack. (Marauders)

ITEMINFO.name: Axe
ITEMINFO.text: Cutting edge: additional save modifier −1 (e.g. S4 with an axe = −2 to the armour save).

ITEMINFO.name: Mace / Hammer / Club
ITEMINFO.text: Concussion: on the injury roll a result of 2–4 is treated as "stunned" — good for knocking opponents out.

ITEMINFO.name: Dagger
ITEMINFO.text: +1 to the armour save of the model hit (6+ if it otherwise has none). The first dagger is free; two weapons give +1 attack.

ITEMINFO.name: Fist
ITEMINFO.text: Only 1 attack; the model hit gets +1 to its armour save. (Only for warriors who have lost their weapon.)

ITEMINFO.name: Gromril armour
ITEMINFO.text: Armour save 4+, with no movement penalty. The best armour in the Old World.

ITEMINFO.name: Ithilmar armour
ITEMINFO.text: Like light armour (save 6+), but with no movement penalty.

ITEMINFO.name: Heavy armour
ITEMINFO.text: Armour save 5+. −1 to movement.

ITEMINFO.name: Light armour
ITEMINFO.text: Armour save 6+. No movement penalty.

ITEMINFO.name: Toughened leather
ITEMINFO.text: Light protection: armour save 6+.

ITEMINFO.name: Sea Dragon cloak
ITEMINFO.text: +2 to the armour save vs shooting, +1 in close combat (combines with armour).

ITEMINFO.name: Enchanted skins
ITEMINFO.text: 6+ ward save, 5+ against magic.

ITEMINFO.name: Buckler
ITEMINFO.text: Parry like a sword (D6 over the to-hit roll, one per phase); no save bonus. With a sword: re-roll one failed parry.

ITEMINFO.name: Shield
ITEMINFO.text: +1 to the armour save (6+ on its own), combines with armour.

ITEMINFO.name: Helmet
ITEMINFO.text: 4+ save against "stunned": a stunned result then becomes only "knocked down".

ITEMINFO.__re: amulet of the moon|amulett des mondes

ITEMINFO.name: Amulet of the Moon
ITEMINFO.text: Enemies −1 to hit with shooting; 5+ ward save against shooting.

ITEMINFO.name: Healing herbs
ITEMINFO.text: Once per battle (at the start of the recovery phase, not in close combat) restores all lost wounds of a model.

ITEMINFO.name: Steel whip
ITEMINFO.text: Cannot be parried: no parries (sword/buckler) are possible against the steel whip. Whipcrack: +1 attack when charging or against a charger (then "strike first"). Sisters of Sigmar only.

ITEMINFO.name: Sigmarite warhammer
ITEMINFO.text: Concussion: injury roll 2–4 = stunned. Holy: +1 on wound rolls against Possessed/Undead. Sisters of Sigmar only.

ITEMINFO.name: Blessed water
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown with BS (no range/movement penalty). Automatically causes 1 wound on Undead/Daemons/Possessed, no armour save. One use.

ITEMINFO.name: Holy relic
ITEMINFO.text: Automatically passes the first Leadership test of the game (for the leader, the first Rout test, provided no Ld test was due earlier). Works only once per game.

ITEMINFO.name: Holy tome
ITEMINFO.text: Warrior Priest / Sister of Sigmar: +1 to the roll for whether a prayer/spell succeeds.

ITEMINFO.name: Hunting arrows
ITEMINFO.text: +1 to all injury rolls.

ITEMINFO.name: Mad cap mushrooms
ITEMINFO.text: Taken before the battle: the model is Frenzied for the whole battle. No effect on Undead/Possessed. Side effect: after the battle roll a D6 — on a 1, permanently stupid.

ITEMINFO.name: Superior black powder
ITEMINFO.text: +1 Strength for all the model's black powder weapons. Lasts one game.

ITEMINFO.name: Dark venom
ITEMINFO.text: Coats a single close-combat weapon. Every hit from the poisoned weapon counts as +1 Strength (also for save modifiers). Not usable with black powder weapons. Lasts one game. Heroes only. (Dark Elves)

ITEMINFO.name: Forest cloak
ITEMINFO.text: Near vegetation (tree/bush/hedge): shooting an additional −1; magic attacks hit the target only on a 4+ — unless the shooter/caster is within his Initiative in inches. Outlaw heroes only.

ITEMINFO.name: Elven cloak
ITEMINFO.text: Shooting at the bearer: −1 to hit (blends with the shadows).

ITEMINFO.name: Elf wine
ITEMINFO.text: Drunk before the battle: the whole warband is immune to fear for the battle. One use.

ITEMINFO.name: Elf runestones
ITEMINFO.text: Wizard: attempt to dispel a spell successfully cast against him or an ally — roll against the spell's difficulty (Sorcery does not help); success = the spell fails.

ITEMINFO.name: Ball and chain
ITEMINFO.text: Goblins only, only together with mad cap mushrooms. Two-handed. Incredible Force: no armour save, a wounding hit causes 1D3 wounds. Cumbersome: no other equipment. Moves randomly (2D6"), on contact with a building/wall immediately OOA; enemies −1 to hit. Ignores Animosity.

ITEMINFO.name: Squig prodder
ITEMINFO.text: In close combat like a spear. Keeps Cave Squigs within 12" (instead of 6") from going wild. Goblins only.

ITEMINFO.name: Standard of Nagarythe
ITEMINFO.text: Shadow Warriors within 6" test Leadership against 10. If the bearer is taken OOA: the whole warband gains Frenzy and may not voluntarily flee. Usable as an improvised spear (−1 to hit). Needs one hand free. Shadow Warriors only.

ITEMINFO.__re: kriegshorn von nagarythe|war horn of nagarythe

ITEMINFO.name: War horn of Nagarythe
ITEMINFO.text: Once per battle at the start of a turn: warband +1 Leadership until the next turn (good before a Rout test). Shadow Warriors only.

ITEMINFO.name: Net
ITEMINFO.text: Once per game throw instead of shooting (BS, no penalties). On a hit roll a D6: ≤ Strength = breaks free; otherwise no movement/shooting/spells in the next turn.

ITEMINFO.name: Warhorse
ITEMINFO.text: Only with the optional rider rules, humans only. The warhorse attacks alongside (WS3, 1 attack). Allows lance/cavalry bonuses.

ITEMINFO.name: Barding
ITEMINFO.text: Mounted model: +1 armour save (so +2 instead of +1 for riding). The mount is killed on OOA only on a 1. Warhorses only.

ITEMINFO.name: Thrown weapons
ITEMINFO.text: Thrown: no penalty for range/movement; cannot be used in close combat.

ITEMINFO.name: Blessed stag hide
ITEMINFO.text: Allows re-rolling one failed Initiative test once per turn. (Horned Hunters, Rare 10)

ITEMINFO.name: Trip wire
ITEMINFO.text: A hero sets a trap (spending a whole turn). A model within 2" of the marker: D6, on a 3+ it triggers → S4 hit. On no wound/no trigger it may move on, otherwise it is knocked down/stunned 2" from the marker. (Master Trapper: 4", triggers on 2+.)

Riesenspinne.label: Giant Spider
Riesenspinne.note: Mount (Blazing Saddles, 1b) — 100 gc, Rare 11, Goblins only. Poisoned attacks: S4, but no armour save modification. Wall Walk: spider and rider climb walls without Initiative tests; rider re-rolls failed climbs; jumps max 2" (counts as diving charge, rider tests Initiative or "Whoa Boy!"). May not be used in a warband that already contains Giant Wolves.

Warhorse.label: Warhorse
Warhorse.note: Mount — the rider uses the Warhorse's Movement and follows the mounted-warrior and "Whoa Boy!" rules. A mounted model cannot enter buildings.

Pferd.label: Riding Horse
Pferd.note: Riding horse — not trained for battle (makes no attacks); used to move quickly. "Whoa Boy!" applies; cannot enter buildings.

Nightmare.label: Nightmare
Nightmare.note: Undead steed — May Not Run (may charge); immune to poison and psychology; if the rider is wounded, roll on the "Whoa Boy!" table.

Warhound.label: Warhound
Warhound.note: War dog — a separate model that fights alongside its owner. Cannot enter buildings.

_FAM.__re: gromril-?waffe|gromril weapon|ithilmar-?waffe|ithilmar weapon|dark-?elf-?klinge|dark elf blade|obsidianwaffe|obsidian weapon
